Wire up via a forge_type enum in config.rs that constructs the right Box<dyn Forge>.","pros":["Follows established, reviewed precedent from PR #736 so reviewers already know the shape","Lowest risk of breaking existing GitHub behavior","Each forge module isolated, easy to test independently","Fast to land incrementally (one forge per PR)"],"cons":["Duplicated boilerplate across four forge modules (client setup, pagination, retry/backoff logic)","Auth handling divergence (Azure AD vs PAT) may force trait to grow optional methods or awkward enums","Harder to keep behavior consistent across forges without a shared base"],"complexity":"medium","files_touched":8},{"name":"Unified REST client with config-driven endpoint/schema mapping","description":"Introduce a generic HttpForgeClient that takes a ForgeSpec (base_url, auth_scheme, endpoint templates, response schema mapping) so GitLab/Gitea/Azure DevOps are configuration instances of one client rather than separate structs. Gitea/Forgejo can even share a spec given their API compatibility with GitHub-style routes.","pros":["Eliminates duplication; new forges added mostly via config/data rather than new code","Naturally captures that Gitea is largely GitHub-API-compatible, reducing to near-zero new code for that provider","Centralizes retry/pagination/rate-limit logic in one place"],"cons":["Higher upfront design cost to build a mapping layer general enough for divergent APIs (Azure DevOps uses very different resource model/versioning)","Riskier refactor touching the existing GitHub implementation to fit the new abstraction","Debugging becomes harder when behavior is data-driven rather than explicit code"],"complexity":"complex","files_touched":14},{"name":"Forge registry with feature-flagged plugins","description":"Add a ForgeRegistry that maps forge_type strings to constructor closures, with each forge implementation gated behind a Cargo feature flag (gitlab, gitea, azure-devops). Core scanner and CLI depend only on the registry and the Forge trait, not concrete types, enabling optional compilation to keep default binary lean.","pros":["Users/distributors can compile only the forges they need, reducing dependency surface and binary size","Clean separation of concerns; new forges can be contributed as near-independent modules/crates over time","Sets up for future community-contributed forge plugins"],"cons":["Feature-flag matrix increases CI complexity (must test all combinations)","Slightly more indirection for a first release when all four forges are likely needed by most users anyway","Requires updates to build docs, CONTRIBUTING.md, and release packaging"],"complexity":"complex","files_touched":12}
